content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended memory architecture flow; The recommended architecture logs incoming User / Tool / Document sources in Source Log, routes them through Memory Router and Write-Time Retrieval, canonicalizes entities, then sends work to Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, and Note Constructor before reconciliation into Postgres + pgvector. Stored memory is maintained by Background Consolidator, Memory Repair Worker, and Retention / Forgetting Worker, and read-time answering flows through Assistant Runtime, Read-Time Retrieval Planner, Postgres + pgvector, and Answer Composer.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура', recommended architecture diagram
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Memory write model separates semantic and maintenance paths; In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, the write model distinguishes two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths: Direct structured path for safely normalized facts, preferences, commitments/tasks, and status changes; Note path for important but not-yet-hard-fact material such as rationale, trade-offs, evolving plans, lessons, document digests, and contextual framing. Consolidation promotes mature notes into structured memory when signal is strong enough, while Repair fixes memory degradation such as duplicates, stale or contradictory items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура', write model subsection
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> indexes_memory_content_for_retrieval; The Retrieval indexes storage tier indexes memory content for retrieval using vector indexes, full-text indexes, and filters.; scope=Applies to the storage-tier architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md.
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Storage tiers in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md; The storage model defines five tiers: Tier A Core / in-context memory for small prompt-ready blocks, Tier B Structured long-term memory for typed objects, Tier C Rich note memory for semantic context, Tier D Raw evidence for immutable source material, and Tier E Retrieval indexes for vector, full-text, and filtered retrieval.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope', storage tiers subsection
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> stores_raw_evidence; The Raw evidence storage tier stores chat turns, tool outputs, and imported records in an immutable or almost immutable layer.; scope=Applies to the storage-tier architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> repairs_memory_degradation; Repair fixes memory degradation such as duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 3
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> improves_memory_reconciliation_and_retrieval; Structured scope improves memory behavior by making reconciliation more predictable, reducing retrieval overgeneralization, and preventing false note conflicts.; scope=Applies to structured scope in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> serves_as_intermediate_semantic_layer; The Rich note memory storage tier serves as an intermediate semantic layer for design direction, rationale, local conclusions, plan fragments, document digests, and lessons.; scope=Applies to the storage-tier architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> converts_mature_notes_to_structured_memory; Consolidation converts mature notes into claims, tasks, profile, or episodes when the signal is strong enough.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 3
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> excludes_from_tier; The Core / in-context memory storage tier excludes all claims, all notes, raw transcript, and long fact lists.; scope=Applies to the storage-tier architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md.
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; Truth state and Retrieval state must stay separate; In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, `Truth state` answers whether memory is current as knowledge, while `Retrieval state` answers how it should be searched and kept in the hot path. They must not be mixed: for example, a note can be `resolved` but still `warm` for rationale retrieval, and a claim can be `superseded` but remain `warm` as historical evidence.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 8. Truth state и retrieval state'
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> primary_storage_backend; The recommended memory architecture uses Postgres + pgvector as the primary storage backend.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 4 recommended architecture
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> can_share_physical_storage_backend; The Retrieval indexes storage tier can physically reside in the same Postgres backend as the rest of storage.; scope=Applies to the storage-tier architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md.
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; Claim vs Note write criteria; In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, write a `Claim` when information is explicit, normalizable, suitable for precise answers, and comparable under predicate policy; write a `Note` when information is important and useful for future retrieval but too context-rich or premature for a hard fact. If one material contains explicit facts/tasks plus rationale/direction, write both.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 7. Когда писать `Claim`, а когда `Note`'
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> should_be_written_when; A Note should be written when information is already important and useful for future retrieval, but too context-rich or too early for a hard fact.; scope=Applies to the memory architecture rules in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> has_constraint; When one material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction, both Claim and Note should be written.; scope=Applies to the memory architecture rules in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> has_goal; The AI agent memory layer is intended to store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 goal of the system
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> has_goal; The AI agent memory layer is intended to support updating knowledge rather than only accumulating it.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 goal of the system
- claim hot-path:claim:<13> has_constraint; Memory v3 architecture treats the four-axis design as necessary to avoid memory becoming either a transcript dump or a brittle fact store.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 2 central architecture idea

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=memory; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=memory; reason=The document is an architecture handoff about the v3 memory layer; existing Memory v3 architecture is the safest match for the memory system being described.
- mention=update; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=CONCEPT; name=Memory update process; reason=The section discusses update as a durable architectural process, and no existing candidate safely matches it.
- mention=retrieval/reconciliation; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=CONCEPT; name=Memory retrieval and reconciliation; reason=The paired concept is central to the section and no existing entity covers reconciliation before update.
- mention=User-facing runtime; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=AGENT; name=User-facing runtime; reason=The section names this as a durable architecture component with responsibilities.
- mention=Memory worker / manager; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=AGENT; name=Memory worker / manager; reason=The section names this as a durable architecture component with responsibilities.
- mention=Storage layer; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<5>; type=SERVICE; name=Storage layer; reason=The section names this as a durable architecture component with responsibilities.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<6>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only .. 10. Разделение ответственности
Document lines: 277-328

## 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only

На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:

1. понять, стоит ли вообще писать память
2. найти похожие или конфликтующие items
3. превратить input в typed candidates
4. применить policy:
   - `insert`
   - `update`
   - `supersede`
   - `retract`
   - `noop`

Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- дублями
- stale preferences
- conflicting notes
- task explosion
- summary spam

## 10. Разделение ответственности

### 10.1 User-facing runtime
Делает:
- диалог
- tool orchestration
- read-time retrieval
- cheap routing / escalation

Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.

### 10.2 Memory worker / manager
Делает:
- routing
- retrieval-before-update
- canonicalization
- note/claim extraction
- reconciliation
- consolidation
- repair
- retention

### 10.3 Storage layer
Отвечает за:
- immutable evidence
- typed tables
- transactional updates
- lineage
- indexes
- audit trail

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Document lines: 277-328

## 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only

На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:

1. понять, стоит ли вообще писать память
2. найти похожие или конфликтующие items
3. превратить input в typed candidates
4. применить policy:
   - `insert`
   - `update`
   - `supersede`
   - `retract`
   - `noop`

Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- дублями
- stale preferences
- conflicting notes
- task explosion
- summary spam

## 10. Разделение ответственности

### 10.1 User-facing runtime
Делает:
- диалог
- tool orchestration
- read-time retrieval
- cheap routing / escalation

Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.

### 10.2 Memory worker / manager
Делает:
- routing
- retrieval-before-update
- canonicalization
- note/claim extraction
- reconciliation
- consolidation
- repair
- retention

### 10.3 Storage layer
Отвечает за:
- immutable evidence
- typed tables
- transactional updates
- lineage
- indexes
- audit trail
